channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Parked right on the corner, no mot, stopping two cars from getting on our drive, 7th July
item.description:
This car was dumped at 2am three days ago. Yes it is opposite the road but it’s a narrow road and unfortunately our drive is brick walled and neither me or my partner can swing our cars onto our drive. Where it’s right on the corner of the road we just can’t reverse or manoeuvre In. We thought it would’ve been moved by now and it hasn’t been. My car is a big disabled car for my elderly dad so we need to be able to get on and off the drive as he can’t walk. The car has no mot either. Met police ref - CAD1847/07jul26

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Village Green Road, Barnes Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lordship Lane( next to Cafe Nero)-Flytipping, 7th July
item.description:
Flytipped items, with sharp edges, blocking the pavement. Needs to be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 3, Lordship Lane, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Garden and household waste dumped on pavement, 7th July
item.description:
Approx four bags of garden waste (no stickers on them to suggest they're being collected today) and a bag of household waste dumped next to the public tree here on Parish Lane

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Parish Lane, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Poo bin., 7th July
item.description:
Poo bin needs empery on the left, entrance by bus stop on Crayfrod way

item.title: Rubbish bin contents piled on grass, 7th July
item.description:
I’ve walked past you tile rubbish twice this morning. Looks like it’s been dumped or a rubbish bin has been emptied onto the park.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): College Road, Plaistow, Greater London

item.title: Bags of household waste left next to public bin, 7th July
item.description:
Two black bags of rubbish dumped next to the bin here on Parish Lane. Already ripped open by foxes.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Parish Lane, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish from bins on street, 7th July
item.description:
Please can this be sorted asap as it’s causing more and more foxes to pull rubbish from the bin store. I just saw the street cleaner on the Main Street and he didn’t come down our road. This is at the end of Chelsworth Drive and further into the grassy areas.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chelsworth Drive, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
This bike has been dumped here blocking the pavement for a week now

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cyprus Road, Edmonton, Greater London

item.title: Outside 154 Maygrove Road, 7th July
item.description:
Its overgrown and the branches are touching customers windows, the branches are also low hanging and also touching the roof of the cars parked nearby.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 120-162, Maygrove Road, West Hampstead, Greater London

item.title: Outside 1 narcissus road, 7th July
item.description:
The tree is leaning quite a lot into the houses over the pavement. It’s a healthy tree that has become untethered from its stake. It needs restaking to ensure it grows in the right way. Thank you!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Narcissus Road, West Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead Fox O/S Moreton flat block on Chislehurst Road Sidcup, 7th July

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Council waste refuse collection did not take place + overfill + foxes, 7th July
item.description:
Using the Thames water road works as an excuse I guess, (though they, managed it last few weeks), the refuse was not collected, 107 overfilled their bins and had black bags on the ground (they're doing DIY renovations), foxes open bag, spill it contents, then poo where they scavenge.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dalberg Road, Brixton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog Urinating on the car from the flat, 7th July
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to make a formal and urgent complaint regarding an ongoing issue that has been causing significant distress to my family and me. I park my vehicle in my designated parking space at Belvedere Park. Directly opposite my parking space is Flat 69, Jutland House, DA17 6FG, located on the fifth floor. The occupants of this flat are regularly allowing their dog to urinate from their balcony/porch, with the urine falling directly onto my car. I first reported this matter to the management company on 15 April 2026. Since then, I have sent numerous follow-up emails and have been informed that warning letters have been issued. Despite this, the behaviour has continued without any improvement. Every morning, my vehicle is covered in dog urine, leaving stains and an unpleasant smell. This has become extremely distressing, particularly as I have two young children who are reluctant to get into the car because of its condition. I use my car daily for the school run, and having to clean the vehicle before using it has become both exhausting and emotionally stressful. I now have to pay for my car to be washed almost every week, only for it to be soiled again the following day. This situation has had a significant impact on my family's wellbeing and is no longer something we can reasonably be expected to tolerate. Out of desperation, I contacted the police. The incident was logged under reference number CAD 5233/19June26. I was advised that, as no criminal offence was taking place, they were unable to attend. However, they directed me to submit a formal complaint to Bexley Council. I have already provided the management company with numerous photographs and a video recorded by my neighbour, who witnessed the incident and can confirm what has been happening. I respectfully request that this matter be treated as a matter of urgency. The ongoing behaviour is unacceptable, unhygienic, and has continued for several months despite repeated complaints. I ask that immediate action is taken to prevent any further incidents and that I am informed of the steps that will be taken to resolve this matter permanently. I look forward to your urgent response.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Little Brights Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins on the common overflowing., 7th July
item.description:
All the bins on Clapham Common North Side are leaking rubbish all over the place. Especially outside Eaton House school.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Long Road, Clapham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hi Jaz, are overgrown at the park and onto the street. Can someone please cut them back?, 7th July
item.description:
Please cut back the hedges in the park.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Burton Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Near my house alley way which is public is full of dirt and weeds, 7th July
item.description:
The public who’s use this alley for crossing are facing a problem because of dirt and weeds my location is 30 Collindale Avenue Erith DA81EE This alley way is just next to my house

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Collindale Avenue, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
